jobText:
Middle Front-End Engineer (React, NextJS)
 віддалено
Required Skills

3+ years of professional front-end development experience
Strong proficiency in TypeScript and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
Solid experience with React 19 (hooks, server components)
Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, SSR, ISR, API routes)
Experience with Tailwind CSS and responsive design principles
Familiarity with component libraries, particularly Radix UI or similar headless UI solutions
Experience with state management solutions (Zustand, Redux, or similar)
Understanding of data fetching patterns (React Query / TanStack Query)
Git version control and collaborative development workflows
Understanding of web performance optimization
Nice to Have

Experience with Framer Motion or other animation libraries
Familiarity with Stripe payment integration
Familiarity with carousel/slider libraries (Swiper, Embla Carousel)
Understanding of accessibility (a11y) best practices
What You’ll Do

Develop and maintain user-facing features using React and Next.js
Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code
Optimize application performance and user experience
Participate in code reviews and contribute to team best practices
Work closely with back-end engineers on API integration
About the Project
We are building an innovative gamified commerce platform that blends e-commerce with interactive, entertainment-style experiences. The product allows users to engage with a fun, surprise-driven purchasing flow, while enabling brands and sellers to manage their own customized storefronts, product catalogs, and analytics through a self-serve dashboard.

must_have: 3+ years of professional front-end development experience
Strong proficiency in TypeScript and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
Solid experience with React 19 (hooks, server components)
Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, SSR, ISR, API routes)
Experience with Tailwind CSS and responsive design principles
Familiarity with component libraries, particularly Radix UI or similar headless UI solutions
Experience with state management solutions (Zustand, Redux, or similar)
Understanding of data fetching patterns (React Query / TanStack Query)
Git version control and collaborative development workflows
nice_to_have: Experience with Framer Motion or other animation libraries
Familiarity with carousel/slider libraries (Swiper, Embla Carousel)
responsibilities: Develop and maintain user-facing features using React and Next.js
Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code
Optimize application performance and user experience
Participate in code reviews and contribute to team best practices
Work closely with back-end engineers on API integration
red_flags: No information on team size or engineering culture depth
Seniority title is 'Middle' but React 19 (very recent) is listed as a hard requirement — may indicate rapidly shifting tech expectations

tailored.headline: Middle Front-End Engineer | React, Next.js, TypeScript
tailored.summary: Front-End Engineer with 5+ years of experience building production web applications using React, Next.js, and TypeScript. Comfortable taking features from design to deployment independently, writing clean and well-tested code, and collaborating closely with backend engineers, designers, and PMs. Experienced with modern data-fetching patterns, state management, and web performance optimization.

bullets: Contributed to building and maintaining an Nx monorepo that unified a marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages, improving UI component reuse across the ecosystem
Developed key marketplace features including secure authentication flows, multi-step order creation, and advanced search with dynamic filtering
Built a real-time P2P chat using WebSockets, enabling live messaging and status synchronization between buyers and sellers within active transactions
Delivered an admin panel with financial dashboards, role-based access control (RBAC), subscription management, and dispute resolution tools
Developed marketing landing pages using Next.js with integrated analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations, contributing to a ~15% improvement in user conversion rates
Participated in code reviews, contributed to CI/CD and monitoring processes, and collaborated with backend engineers to align on API contracts and delivery timelines
Worked closely with PMs, BAs, and designers to break down requirements into well-scoped, deliverable front-end tasks
Developed an SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js (SSR, App Router), contributing to improved B2B user acquisition through better page speed and search rankings
Stabilized the existing React-based admin dashboard by auditing and fixing critical bugs in business logic, broken API requests, and improper error handling
Integrated Stripe to implement a subscription and billing system, including multi-tier pricing pages, billing management UI, and transaction status workflows
Built an embeddable chat widget using Shadow DOM for clean DOM isolation and WebSockets for real-time AI-powered responses on client websites
Developed an Integrations Hub allowing users to connect third-party platforms (Slack, Telegram, Webhooks) to route chatbot leads and notifications
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query, establishing consistent caching, background revalidation, and state synchronization patterns
Improved client-side performance (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing bundle size by ~10% and widget load times by ~15%
Integrated Sentry for error tracking and production monitoring, reducing debugging time and improving application observability
Contributed to building and launching the platform from scratch, delivering features across the admin panel and marketing modules
Developed an SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts
Built an interactive Widget Builder (Alert Box, Goal Progress) allowing streamers to customize colors, layouts, sounds, typography, and animation triggers for OBS overlays
Implemented OAuth integrations with Twitch, YouTube, and Kick for streamer authorization, profile sync, and live broadcast status tracking
Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive data tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts
Collaborated with backend engineers to integrate REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux and real-time alert distribution
Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs using SCSS, ensuring cross-browser compatibility and responsive behavior across all device sizes

coverage.requirement: 3+ years of professional front-end development experience
coverage.evidence: 5+ years across DonateSystem (2020–2022), AMO FINTECH (2022–2025), and KYC Market (2025–Present)

coverage.requirement: Strong proficiency in TypeScript and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
coverage.evidence: TypeScript listed as primary language across all 3 roles

coverage.requirement: Solid experience with React 19 (hooks, server components)
coverage.evidence: React is used across all roles; React 19 specifically is not mentioned in the master CV — hooks and component patterns are implied but React 19 version is not confirmed

coverage.requirement: Hands-on experience with Next.js (App Router, SSR, ISR, API routes)
coverage.evidence: Next.js used at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH; SSR and marketing landing pages with Next.js explicitly mentioned

coverage.requirement: Experience with Tailwind CSS and responsive design principles
coverage.evidence: Tailwind CSS listed in skills and used at AMO FINTECH; responsive design mentioned at DonateSystem

coverage.requirement: Familiarity with component libraries, particularly Radix UI or similar headless UI solutions
coverage.evidence: Material UI used at AMO FINTECH; Radix UI not mentioned — headless UI experience is partial

coverage.requirement: Experience with state management solutions (Zustand, Redux, or similar)
coverage.evidence: Zustand at KYC Market, Redux Toolkit and Redux at DonateSystem, all explicitly listed

coverage.requirement: Understanding of data fetching patterns (React Query / TanStack Query)
coverage.evidence: TanStack Query used and refactored at AMO FINTECH and KYC Market; caching and revalidation patterns described

coverage.requirement: Git version control and collaborative development workflows
coverage.evidence: Code review workflows, CI/CD participation, and cross-team collaboration mentioned across roles
LCP/TTI improvements, code-splitting, lazy loading, bundle size reduction (~10%) at AMO FINTECH; Next.js SSR for page speed at KYC Market

coverage.requirement: Experience with Framer Motion or other animation libraries

coverage.evidence: Stripe integration implemented at AMO FINTECH — subscription billing, pricing pages, transaction status workflows

coverage.requirement: Familiarity with carousel/slider libraries (Swiper, Embla Carousel)

tailored.match_notes:
**Strong match overall.** Your React, Next.js, TypeScript, Zustand, TanStack Query, Stripe, and performance optimization experience maps directly to the core requirements. The Stripe integration alone is a meaningful differentiator for this role.

**Real gaps to be aware of:**
- **React 19 specifically** — your CV does not confirm you have worked with React 19 (server components, new hooks API). If you have not, be ready to address this in an interview. It is listed as a hard requirement but is very recent, so many candidates will be in the same position.
- **Radix UI / headless UI** — you have Material UI experience, but Radix UI is not in your CV. Worth mentioning Material UI as a comparable component library and studying Radix UI basics before interviews.
- **Framer Motion** — not in your background at all. Minor gap since it is nice-to-have.
- **Swiper / Embla Carousel** — not mentioned. Also nice-to-have, low risk.
- **Accessibility (a11y)** — not explicitly covered in your CV. If you have applied semantic HTML or ARIA attributes in practice, consider mentioning it.

**Seniority note:** Your experience is genuinely above Middle level. This CV is intentionally framed to match the role's Middle seniority expectations, but your real background is Strong Middle / Middle+. That is a good position — you will likely stand out among Middle candidates without appearing overqualified.

keyword_coverage.location: headline, summary, top_skills, experience (AMO FINTECH bullet 1, DonateSystem bullet 2, KYC Market bullet 5)
summary (production web applications — partial); headline (Front-End Engineer)

format_checks.rule: All experience bullets start with an action verb
format_checks.note: Two bullets open with 'Contributed to' — KYC Market bullet 1 ('Contributed to building and maintaining…') and DonateSystem bullet 1 ('Contributed to building and launching…'). 'Contributed to' is a weak, passive-sounding opener that many ATS parsers and screeners flag alongside 'Helped with'. Replace with a direct action verb that names the concrete output (e.g. 'Architected', 'Launched', 'Delivered').

format_checks.rule: No passive constructions (Responsible for / Helped with)
Standard sections present (headline, summary, skills, experience, education)
No special characters or table-like structures that break ATS parsers
Job title in headline matches target role title exactly
